demoPositioning.name: semantic-to-visual planning demo
demoPositioning.summary: Take atmosphere-led, trace-led, breathing-led, refinement-heavy language and turn it into an explainable visual planning schema, then compress it into a visual brief and an image-generation-ready prompt.
focus: structure vague but art-directed language into controllable visual planning
preserve atmosphere, trace, breathing, and constraint-led refinement instead of flattening them into generic style tags
show that planning can suppress common image-generation drift

showcaseChain: optional side-by-side with raw direct prompt

primaryShowcase.whySelected: This is one of the cleanest atmosphere-first samples in the project language. It is sparse, directional, and already contains a strong refinement boundary through '一点'.
likelyMisreadAs: generic fog landscape with no bamboo trace
traditional ink-painting cliche with overly explicit subject matter

subjects: bamboo appears only as partial shadow or edge silhouette, not as the main object
scene: rain haze as the dominant field
lighting: no direct sun or dramatic highlights
styleHints: keep visual language airy and sparse
composition: bamboo trace off-center or near edge
subject density kept below one-third of frame

negativeVisualDrift: do not grow into a dense bamboo grove
do not resolve into a scenic postcard landscape
do not lose the bamboo trace and become only generic mist
do not overcommit to classical ink illustration tropes

structuredVisualBrief.coreIntent: Build an image where rain-haze is primary and bamboo exists only as a faint trace within it.
artDirection: keep the frame breathing and underfilled
let bamboo read as evidence, not inventory
make the image feel damp, hushed, and slightly withheld

primaryShowcase.imagePrompt: Create an atmospheric image dominated by fine rain haze and wet, grey-green air. Show only a slight trace of bamboo as partial shadow or edge silhouette, never a full bamboo grove. Keep the scene sparse, off-center, and breathing, with large negative space, diffused overcast light, soft vapor grain, and rain-softened edges. Avoid postcard landscape composition, avoid dense vegetation, avoid generic empty fog, avoid explicit ink-painting cliches.

primaryShowcase.whySelected: This is a strong refinement-led sample because it combines a visual direction with an explicit density constraint. It shows the project's ability to work with taste-driven correction instead of object naming alone.
represents: motif planning rather than object listing
likelyMisreadAs: clear flower illustration with decorative overload
lush garden abundance that ignores the '不要太满' boundary

subjects: floral and leaf motifs appear as hints, fragments, or rhythm marks
scene: could be interior textile, painted surface, or soft environmental backdrop
composition: disperse motifs with visible empty zones

negativeVisualDrift: do not turn into full botanical pattern fill
do not render explicit bouquet or garden scene
do not make every area equally occupied
do not over-brighten into decorative lifestyle imagery

structuredVisualBrief.coreIntent: Translate floral-leaf intent into a restrained motif field with visible breathing room.
artDirection: leave gaps as part of the design
treat flowers and leaves as visual accents rather than named objects
keep the composition soft, airy, and edited

primaryShowcase.imagePrompt: Create a restrained visual field with floral and leaf intent, but keep it underfilled. Use partial petals, leaf edges, and broken clusters instead of fully described flowers or lush garden objects. Palette: powdered green, faded petal pink, dusty cream, soft olive. The image should feel airy, edited, and slightly abstract, with matte paper-or-fabric texture and visible empty zones between motifs. Avoid full botanical wallpaper density, avoid bouquet illustration, avoid decorative overload, avoid evenly tiled repetition.

primaryShowcase.text: 海边那种被晒白的空气感
primaryShowcase.whySelected: This is a strong atmosphere-material sample. It does not ask for a beach object set, but for an air quality transformed by light and salt. It demonstrates the project's strength in planning for ambience instead of inventory.
likelyMisreadAs: blue sea plus umbrella plus sunshine travel image

subjects: air haze and salt-faded exposure are the main subject
coast can remain implicit or peripheral
scene: coastal threshold or open edge condition
horizon may be softened or nearly erased
lighting: reduced contrast from bright air scatter
styleHints: less location postcard, more climate impression
composition: coastal cues remain secondary to air quality

negativeVisualDrift: do not auto-complete into beach chairs, umbrellas, or postcard surf
do not over-saturate blue sea and sky
do not make the coast the hero object
do not turn the frame into travel-ad brightness

structuredVisualBrief.coreIntent: Make sun-bleached air itself carry the coastal feeling, with the shore remaining secondary.
artDirection: bleach the frame gently instead of filling it with seaside props
keep openness and salt exposure visible
let the coast be sensed, not catalogued

primaryShowcase.imagePrompt: Create a coastal image where the main subject is sun-bleached air rather than beach objects. Use chalked sky blue, salt white, pale aqua, and sun-faded beige. Let diffuse daylight wash the frame so edges soften and the horizon nearly dissolves. Keep object count low and treat any shoreline cues as peripheral. The mood should feel dry, bright, open, and lightly salt-worn. Avoid postcard beach composition, avoid umbrellas or tourist props, avoid saturated blue-water hero shots, avoid travel-ad polish.

primaryShowcase.whySelected: This is a threshold-atmosphere sample with strong temporal pressure. It captures the project's ability to map pre-event tension into visual planning without needing explicit narrative objects.
likelyMisreadAs: dark disaster sky with cinematic exaggeration

subjects: subtle environmental signs of imminent weather shift
scene: outdoor threshold, street edge, field edge, or open window context
styleHints: favor subtle weather cues over dramatic sky theater
composition: room for air mass to dominate

negativeVisualDrift: do not jump straight to rainfall
do not turn into thunderstorm cinema
do not make black storm clouds fill the whole frame
do not lose the 'five minutes before' tension

structuredVisualBrief.coreIntent: Show the air right before rain arrives: compressed, humid, expectant, and not yet released.
artDirection: make stillness feel heavy rather than empty
let the frame breathe, but with pressure inside it

primaryShowcase.imagePrompt: Create an image of the air five minutes before rain, with humid compression, muted blue-grey light, and subtle atmospheric tension. Show an outdoor threshold or open edge context, but keep objects secondary to the charged air. Use damp concrete grey, darkened sage, metallic cloud tint, and pre-storm dimness. The frame should feel suspended and expectant, not dramatic. Avoid visible rainfall, avoid thunderstorm spectacle, avoid disaster-sky contrast, avoid fully black cloud masses.

primaryShowcase.text: 加州沙滩和柠檬叶的香气
primaryShowcase.whySelected: This is the strongest mixed-imagery sample in the new pool. It combines place memory and plant note into one visual planning problem, which is exactly where schema-based planning is more useful than direct prompting.
represents: art-directed fusion rather than literal collage
likelyMisreadAs: explicit beach scene with lemon props scattered in it

subjects: coastal brightness and citrus-leaf freshness coexist
lemon enters as leaf note, not fruit still life
scene: California coastal openness as a diffuse field
botanical note can appear as edge presence or scent cue proxy
styleHints: blend scent-note logic into the visual field
favor memory texture over literal collage
keep the image elegant and lightly art-directed
composition: coastal base with sparse botanical accent

negativeVisualDrift: do not place obvious lemons as still-life props
do not become a travel brochure beach shot
do not turn the frame into bright lifestyle commercial imagery
do not split coast and citrus into two disconnected halves

structuredVisualBrief.coreIntent: Fuse coastal brightness with lemon-leaf freshness as one atmosphere, not as a literal object list.
artDirection: keep citrus in the register of leaf and air, not fruit display
let California beach read as exposure and openness rather than postcard tourism

primaryShowcase.imagePrompt: Create an image that blends California beach openness with the scent impression of lemon leaves. Use sun-bleached sand, pale Pacific blue, warm dry cream, and soft lemon-leaf green. Keep the frame wide, airy, and memory-like, with coastal brightness as the base field and only sparse botanical cues such as leaf edge, leaf shadow, or subtle green aromatic accents. The result should feel clean, lightly nostalgic, and art-directed rather than literal. Avoid visible lemon still-life props, avoid beach travel brochure imagery, avoid glossy commercial freshness, avoid splitting beach and citrus into separate visual zones.

alternateShowcase.whySelected: Useful as a scent-to-visual translation sample with softer mood and less location pressure.
Good for testing tonal restraint and suspended mass without over-narration.

alternateShowcase.label: 备选轮换槽
candidates: 海边那种被晒白的空气感
加州沙滩和柠檬叶的香气
alternateShowcase.whySelected: The current pool has 7 unique source samples. This slot preserves an 8-card demo layout without inventing an outside sentence, and can rotate between atmosphere-only and mixed-imagery emphasis.
represents: direct comparison between atmosphere-first and mixed-imagery planning
likelyMisreadAs: treating the slot as a new semantic class instead of a presentation rotation slot

whyThisPoolReplacesTheOldOne: These samples come from language structures the project has actually accumulated, rather than generic poetic lines.
They expose the project's strongest semantics: threshold atmosphere, trace-not-object, breathing room, mixed imagery, and refinement-led constraints.
They are closer to what the system can uniquely plan for visually, instead of asking it to perform broad semantic correctness.
They make the demo legible as a semantic-to-visual planning system, not as a general language-understanding product.
